summary: Completed comprehensive testing of DressApp's new Add Item feature including image analysis, marketplace intent functionality, and frontend UI components

flaky_endpoints.issue: Gemini 2.5 Pro analysis sometimes returns incomplete data (missing category field)
flaky_endpoints.symptoms: Returns title but not all expected fields like category
flaky_endpoints.workaround: Retry analysis or provide fallback values

flaky_endpoints.symptoms: Service unavailable errors during peak usage

budget_issues.issue: Budget exceeded for stylist calendar integration
budget_issues.symptoms: litellm.BadRequestError: Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 6.17, Max budget: 5.00

passed_tests: Backend: Marketplace intent 'own' creates private items only
Backend: Marketplace intent 'for_sale' creates both closet item and listing with 7% platform fee
Backend: Marketplace intent 'donate' creates listing with mode='donate' and price=0
Backend: Marketplace intent 'swap' creates listing with mode='swap'
Backend: Schema fields round-trip correctly (colors[], fabric_materials[], season[], tags[])
Backend: Provider activity tracking records garment-vision calls
Backend: Analyze endpoint validation (400 for missing/invalid inputs, 401 for no auth)
Frontend: Add Item page loads correctly with dropzone
Frontend: File input properly hidden but accessible (sr-only class)
Frontend: Navigation buttons (Back, Add More, Save All) present and functional
Frontend: Save All button initially disabled
Frontend: Responsive design works on desktop, tablet, and mobile
Frontend: Accessibility features (ARIA attributes, proper color contrast)
Frontend: CSS animations and reduced motion support
Frontend: Marketplace integration - items with marketplace intent appear in /market page
Frontend: Market page displays listings created via Add Item flow
action_item_for_main_agent: Minor issues: 1) Gemini analysis occasionally returns incomplete data - consider adding fallback logic for missing fields. 2) Image URL analysis endpoint returns 503 errors - investigate service availability. 3) Consider increasing LLM budget if calendar integration is needed for testing.
